article_meta.title: New York Playbook POLITICO's must-read briefing informing the daily conversation among knowledgeable New Yorkers New Yor

reasoning: The model returned an empty array despite multiple clear instances of author-level bias in the prose. Examples include promotional framing ("must-read briefing"), loaded/subjective characterizations ("highlighting the murky ethics," "putting him in an uncomfortable position with the paper that's pulled its punches with him"), and evaluative asides ("isn't true blue though"). None of these are quotes, and they reflect spin/word choice and subjective judgments by the authors.
